content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


September 25, 2001


Senator K. Maurice Johannessen
State Capitol, Room 5061
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No.   A-01-207

Dear Senator Johannessen:

       This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). ¹

QUESTION

       Are your investment accounts over which an investment manager has full
discretion disclosable under the Act?

CONCLUSION

       Yes. Each investment held in a fully managed account must be reported
independently on your Form 700.

FACTS

       You have two accounts held at Morgan Stanley in the name of Regent Investor
Services and Harris Bretall Sullivan & Smith L.L.C. You signed a power of attorney
giving the investment manager full discretion over what to buy, when to buy, what to sell
and when to sell, just like a mutual fund. Currently, your Regent account has stock in 60
different business entities ranging in value from a low of $542 to a high of $7,596. The
Harris account has stock in 40 different business entities ranging in value from a low of
$724 to a high of $7,634.

_______________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-01-206
Page No. 2

As with mutual funds, both of these managers are Registered Investment
Advisors, and they provide diversified portfolios. The reason they are growing in
popularity is that, unlike a mutual fund, they provide each client with actual cost basis
that is important in taxable investment accounts.

ANALYSIS

As Senator for the 4th Senate District, you must file a Form 700 each year.
(Section 87200.) On the Form 700, you disclose, among other things, your
"investments," as defined in the Act. (Section 87206.)

The Act defines "investment" to include, among other things, any financial
interest in or security issued by a business entity. (Section 82034.) However, the
definition of investment also includes several exceptions. You have asked about one of
these exceptions. Specifically, an "interest in a diversified mutual fund registered with
the Securities and Exchange Commission under the Investment Company Act of 1940" is
not considered an "investment" for purposes of the Act. (Ibid.)

You analogize your fully managed accounts to a diversified mutual fund, on the
ground that an investor in a diversified mutual fund has no control over the fund's
investments, just as you do not control the stock held in your name in each of these
accounts. Based on that analogy, you ask if the exception for diversified mutual funds
covers your fully managed accounts.

The plain language of Section 82034 compels the conclusion that your
investments in the fully managed accounts are fully reportable as distinct investments.
This issue has previously been addressed in the Gibson Advice Letter, No. A-00-026.
There, we concluded that although one could analogize between a fully managed account
and a diversified mutual fund, only diversified mutual funds registered with the SEC
under the Investment Company Act of 1940 are covered by the exception. Since your
fully managed accounts do not meet those criteria, the exception does not apply to your
accounts. Thus, you must report on your Form 700, each investment held in the fully
managed accounts according to Section 87206. Please note that the disclosure threshold
is $2,000. (Section 87206(d).)

File No. A-01-206
Page No. 3

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Natalie Bocanegra
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

NB:jg

QUESTION: Are your investment accounts over which an investment manager has full
discretion disclosable under the Act?

CONCLUSION: Yes. Each investment held in a fully managed account must be reported
independently on your Form 700.
